items.text:
1. `lume entities`: prints the entity kind names from `validate.entity_kinds()`
to stdout, one per line. Exit 0. No workstream target required.
(Evidence: test asserts all three names in output; exit code 0.)

2. `lume schema <entity>`: prints the JSON Schema for `entity` as
pretty-printed JSON (indent=2, sorted keys) to stdout. Exit 0. Unknown
entity exits 1 with a named error to stderr (entity name + known set listed).
No workstream target required. (Evidence: known entity outputs valid JSON
with "title"; unknown entity exits 1 with stderr mentioning known set.)

3. `lume get [-w <slug>]` (no entity): emits the full state document as
formatted JSON (`state.dumps()`). Resolves workstream via `-w`/default-active.
Exit 0. (Evidence: output is valid JSON containing "workstream", "iterations",
"plan" keys.)

4. `lume get [-w <slug>] <entity>` (entity, no id): emits the corresponding
top-level value — the workstream entity for `workstream`, the full iterations
array for `iteration`, the full plan array for `plan_item`. Unknown entity
exits 1 to stderr. (Evidence: workstream emits a dict; iteration emits an
array; unknown exits 1.)

5. `lume get [-w <slug>] <entity> <id>` (entity + id): finds the matching
element by `id` field and emits it as formatted JSON. Iteration id matched
as integer (leading zeros stripped: "007" → 7). Plan_item id matched as
string ("P1"). `workstream` with an id exits 1 (usage error). Not-found
exits 1 with a named error. (Evidence: `get iteration 007` emits the entity;
`get plan_item P1` emits the item; not-found exits 1.)

6. All three verbs are read-only: no `state.save()`, no markdown write, no
mutation method called. (Evidence: implementation has no save/write calls in
the new verb paths.)

7. Full suite green; ≥ 6 new tests covering entities output, schema known +
unknown (exit codes), get full state, get entity (workstream + array kinds),
get entity+id (found + not-found), workstream-with-id exits 1. No tests
removed. (Evidence: 138 tests, 0 failures.)

dod.preamble:
Three new read-only verbs. None mutate state.json or any markdown file. No
workstream target needed for `entities` or `schema`; `get` resolves the workstream
via the normal `-w`/default-active path.

Entity-to-state-key mapping (entity_kinds → state doc field):
  `workstream` → state["workstream"]  (singular object; no id selector)
  `iteration`  → state["iterations"] (array; id = integer, e.g. 007 or 7)
  `plan_item`  → state["plan"]        (array; id = string, e.g. P1)

handback:
P6 complete. All DoD items satisfied. Full suite green (138/138). No markdown
written, no state mutated by any discovery verb. Ready for verdict.
self_review:
All three verbs implemented and wired in `cli.py`. The `entities`/`schema` branch
runs before workstream resolution so no target is needed. `get` resolves the
workstream normally then reads `ws.state_doc` without calling any mutating method.
Iteration id normalisation (`"007"` → `7` via `lstrip("0") or "0"`) handles both
padded and bare forms. 20 new tests (3 entities, 5 schema, 12 get); 138 total, all
green.
